metadata.name: Dyscalculia Support Skill
metadata.description: Accessibility tools for number sense, math visualization, and step-by-step calculation support tailored to dyscalculic users
metadata.author: Justice

skills.name: dyscalculia-support
skills.description: Knowledge about dyscalculia, number processing challenges, subitizing difficulties, spatial reasoning, and evidence-based interventions for mathematical accessibility

commands.name: number-visualization
commands.description: Create visual representations of numbers, quantities, and mathematical relationships using concrete models, number lines, and area models

expression.description: The number, quantity, or math expression to visualize

model_type.description: Visualization model (number-line, area-model, base-ten-blocks, fraction-bar, pie-chart)

include_labels.description: Whether to include descriptive labels and annotations

commands.name: step-by-step-math
commands.description: Break down a math problem into explicit, scaffolded steps with plain-language explanations and intermediate results at each stage

problem.description: The math problem to solve step by step

show_check.description: Whether to include a verification/check step at the end

use_analogies.description: Whether to include real-world analogies for abstract concepts

commands.name: estimation-practice
commands.description: Generate estimation exercises that build number sense and magnitude awareness using everyday contexts

topic.description: Topic area for estimation exercises (money, time, distance, weight, quantity)

commands.name: range-comparison
commands.description: Compare two or more datasets visually using simplified range bars with central-value markers, designed to avoid the cognitive overload of comparing raw statistics numerically

datasets.description: Datasets to compare, each as 'Label: min-max, center' (e.g., 'Class A: 40-95, 72'). Center value can be mean, median, or mode.

center_type.description: What the center marker represents (mean, median, mode)

show_numbers.description: Whether to annotate the center value as a number next to the marker (false = purely visual comparison)

context.description: Optional real-world context to frame the comparison (e.g., 'test scores', 'temperatures in °C', 'prices in $')

commands.name: proportion-waffle
commands.description: Visualize proportions and percentages using a 10×10 waffle grid of countable squares instead of pie charts, making part-whole relationships concrete and subitizable

items.description: Proportions to display, each as 'Label: value' (e.g., 'Passed: 72%', 'Boys: 15 out of 30'). Accepts percentages, fractions, or 'X out of Y' formats.

total.description: The whole that proportions are part of (default: auto-detect from input, or 100 for percentages)

grid_size.description: Grid dimension (default 10 = 10×10 = 100 squares). Use 5 for simpler visuals with younger learners.

side_by_side.description: Whether to show multiple proportions as separate grids side by side for comparison

commands.name: trend-direction
commands.description: Show whether values are rising, falling, or flat over time using simplified directional indicators and optional sparklines, without requiring axis reading

series.description: Data series as 'Label: v1, v2, v3, ...' (e.g., 'Weekly scores: 65, 70, 68, 75, 80'). At least 3 values needed to determine trend.

show_sparkline.description: Whether to show a mini sparkline curve alongside the direction arrow (no axes, just shape)

sensitivity.description: How much change counts as a trend vs. flat (strict, moderate, loose)

context.description: Optional context for the plain-language summary (e.g., 'math quiz scores over 6 weeks')

commands.name: difference-gap
commands.description: Visualize the difference between two values as an explicit gap bar, making 'how much more/less' visible without requiring mental subtraction

show_gap_value.description: Whether to label the gap with the numeric difference (false = just show the visual gap)

commands.name: elapsed-time
commands.description: Calculate and visualize the time between two points using a timeline bar, bypassing the need for mental base-60 arithmetic on clock values

show_clock_faces.description: Whether to show simple clock face diagrams at start and end points

break_into_steps.description: Whether to break the elapsed time into hour and minute chunks visually (e.g., '1 hour + 35 minutes' shown as labeled segments)
